section.name: models
section.summary: Claude model tiers (2025-2026): Haiku 3.5 for speed/cost, Sonnet 4 for balanced production, Opus 4.5 for complex reasoning and Extended Thinking.

list.item: claude-3-5-haiku-20241022 — $0.80/$4.00 per 1M tokens; 200K context; fastest; use for classification, extraction, FAQ
claude-sonnet-4-20250514 — $3.00/$15.00 per 1M tokens; 200K context; default choice for generation, code, RAG
claude-opus-4-5-20251101 — $15.00/$75.00 per 1M tokens; 200K context; Extended Thinking; use for deep reasoning, contract analysis, research synthesis

section.name: messages-api
section.summary: Primary API for all Claude interactions. Single endpoint: POST /v1/messages. Handles single-turn, multi-turn, tool use, vision, and streaming.
section.rule: Always pass max_tokens explicitly: 512 for Haiku classification, 4096 for Sonnet generation, 8192+ for Opus extended reasoning. Default behavior is model-dependent and can result in truncated outputs.
Use the Python SDK (pip install anthropic) or TypeScript SDK (npm install @anthropic-ai/sdk). Do not hand-roll HTTP calls — the SDKs handle retries, streaming SSE parsing, and version headers.
Load ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from the environment, never hardcode. In production use a secrets manager (1Password, AWS Secrets Manager, Vault).

example.code:
import anthropic client = anthropic.Anthropic() # reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env
response = client.messages.create( model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=1024, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Summarize this document."}], ) print(response.content[0].text)

section.name: tool-use
section.summary: Claude selects and calls tools by returning tool_use blocks. The agent loop appends results as tool_result blocks and continues until stop_reason == "end_turn".
section.rule: Always enforce a MAX_TURNS cap (recommended: 10). An agent without it can loop indefinitely when Claude never reaches end_turn due to a misbehaving tool or prompt.
Use forced tool use (tool_choice={"type": "tool", "name": "output_schema"}) for structured JSON output. It is more reliable than prompting for JSON across model updates.
Provide fewer than 10 tools simultaneously. Tool use reliability degrades with large tool sets — curate the tool list per task rather than passing the full registry.
Validate tool inputs against the tool's input_schema before executing. Schema drift between Claude's call and the handler's expectations causes silent runtime errors.

example.description: Tool use loop with MAX_TURNS guard

section.name: prompt-caching
section.summary: Marks content blocks with cache_control to reuse them across requests. Cache hit: 90% cost reduction on input tokens. TTL: 5 minutes, extended on each use. Minimum cacheable block: 1,024 tokens.
section.rule: Always cache system prompts and large static context (retrieved documents, tool schemas) that appear in multiple requests. Blocks under 1,024 tokens are silently not cached — do not assume caching on short prompts.
Cache the longest stable prefix first. Cached blocks must appear in the same order and at the same position across requests — any change before the cached block invalidates the cache.

Prompt Caching is ineffective for low-traffic applications where the 5-minute TTL expires between requests. Profile cache hit rate via usage.cache_read_input_tokens before committing to a caching strategy.

example.code:
SYSTEM = [ { "type": "text", "text": LARGE_SYSTEM_PROMPT, # must be >= 1024 tokens "cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral"}, } ]
response = client.messages.create( model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=2048, system=SYSTEM, messages=messages, ) # Check cache hit in usage print(response.usage.cache_read_input_tokens) # > 0 means cache hit print(response.usage.cache_creation_input_tokens) # > 0 means cache write

section.name: extended-thinking
section.summary: Extended Thinking lets Claude reason step-by-step before answering, improving accuracy on math, logic, research synthesis, and code debugging. Available on Opus 4.5 only. Minimum budget: 1,024 tokens.
section.rule: Start with a small budget (1,024-5,000 tokens). Increase incrementally until accuracy plateaus — thinking tokens are billed at Opus input rate ($15/1M).

Always check for thinking blocks before text blocks when iterating response.content. The SDK delivers them as thinking_delta events during streaming — if you only listen for text_delta, you pay for thinking tokens silently.
Do not use Extended Thinking for tasks that need very short outputs (under 200 tokens) — the minimum 1,024-token budget makes it economically wasteful.

section.name: streaming
section.summary: Server-sent events (SSE) for real-time token delivery. Python SDK: client.messages.stream() context manager. TypeScript SDK: client.messages.stream(). Event types: message_start, content_block_delta (text_delta / input_json_delta), message_stop.
section.rule: Process content_block_delta events incrementally. Accumulate input_json_delta for tool inputs until the block closes — do not parse partial JSON mid-stream.
On disconnection, resume from the last text block index. The SDK does not handle reconnection automatically — track the last delivered text index and skip already-delivered blocks on reconnect.

example.code: with client.messages.stream( model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=1024, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}], ) as stream: for text in stream.text_stream: print(text, end="", flush=True)

section.name: batch-api
section.summary: Submit up to 10,000 requests as a batch. 50% cost savings vs synchronous. 24-hour processing window. Results via polling or webhook. Use only for offline workloads.
section.rule: Never route user-facing interactive requests through the Batch API. The 24-hour window is incompatible with real-time response expectations.
Group requests by deadline. Offline enrichment (document tagging, summarization, embeddings generation) always goes through Batch. Anything with a user waiting never does.
Poll for completion using client.beta.messages.batches.retrieve(batch_id). Check processing_status == "ended" before streaming results. Do not assume completion before polling confirms it.
